mainmatter.p: A carrable map \({E}\xrightarrow{p}{B}\) in a category \(\mathcal{C}\) is said to be exponentiable when it is exponentiable when viewed as an object of the slice category \({\mathcal{C}}_{/B}\).

A morphism \({E}\xrightarrow{p}{B}\) in a category \(\mathcal{C}\) is said to be carrable when for any \({A}\xrightarrow{f}{B}\), the pullback \({E\times_{B}A}\xrightarrow{f^*p}{A}\) depicted below exists.

Let \(E\) be an object in a category \(\mathcal{C}\) such that all binary cartesian products with \(E\) exist. We say that \(E\) is exponentiable when the product endofunctor \(-\times E\) on \(\mathcal{C}\) has a right adjoint, which we shall write \(E\Rightarrow-\).

A category with representable maps or CwR is defined by Uemura to be a category \(\mathcal{C}\) with finite limits equipped with a pullback-stable class \(\mathrm{R}_{\mathcal{C}}\subseteq \mathbf{hom}_{\mathcal{C}}\) of exponentiable arrows in \(\mathcal{C}\).
